turns.user_message: I tried taking a long walk just to clear my head, but it barely made a dent. I used to be able to shake off this heavy feeling after just ten minutes outside. Now I just pace around my room while my chest stays tight and restless. Every little noise seems to echo louder than it should. I keep telling myself it is just a rough patch, but the usual tricks feel completely hollow.

I tried taking a long walk again yesterday, hoping it would finally reset my system, but my legs just felt heavy and useless. I used to breeze through these moments without a second thought, but lately the tension just clings to my ribs. I sit down to read or watch something, yet my pulse keeps jumping at random. It is exhausting trying to force myself back to normal when nothing actually works. I keep wondering why my own mind decided to turn every quiet moment into a battleground.

I tried forcing myself outside for another walk, but the fresh air just made my breathing feel shallow and frantic. I used to handle these waves so easily, but now they crash over me until I am completely drained. My hands shake even when I am sitting perfectly still, and my thoughts keep spinning out of control. I keep trying every single trick I know, yet nothing stops the sudden rush of dread that hits out of nowhere. It feels like my own nervous system has completely forgotten how to settle down, and I am just riding out the storm.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens with a failed attempt to feel better. This used to not bother the speaker.
